3494	\begin{lemma}[An epimorphism of sheaves is locally surjective]
3495	  \label{lem:sheaf_locally_surjective_iff_epi}
3496	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi'}
3497	  \mathlibok
3498	  \uses{lem:presheaf_is_locally_surjective}
3499	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3500	  A morphism of sheaves (of abelian groups, valued in a suitable category) is an epimorphism in the
3501	  category of sheaves if and only if it is locally surjective. In particular an epimorphism of
3502	  abelian sheaves on the space \(X\) is locally surjective, supplying the covering with local lifts

3653	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
3654	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Writing \(M = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global sections,
3655	  there is a natural isomorphism \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{M}\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules,
3656	  under which \(\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F}) = M_f\) for every \(f \in R\); in particular the sections of
3657	  \(\mathcal{F}\) over a distinguished open \(D(f)\) are the away localisation \(M_f\). This is the
3658	  affine quasi-coherent structure theorem (Tag 01HV, with the equivalence quasi-coherent-on-affine
3659	  \(\simeq\) \(R\)-modules, Tag 01I8).

3663	  On an affine scheme the global-sections functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) and
3664	  the tilde functor \(M \mapsto \widetilde{M}\) are mutually inverse equivalences between
3665	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and \(R\)-modules; the counit
3666	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})} \to \mathcal{F}\) is an isomorphism for quasi-coherent
3667	  \(\mathcal{F}\). By Tag 01HV the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(f)\) are \(M_f\), so the
3668	  isomorphism identifies \(\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) with \(M_f\).

3671	  \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma} : \widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})} \to \mathcal{F}\) is known to
3672	  be an isomorphism, the asserted iso \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})}\) is its
3673	  inverse (the one-line conditional argument formalized). The hypothesis that the counit is an
3674	  isomorphism holds for any \(\mathcal{F}\) admitting a global presentation

3870	  construction. Finally a finite family of basic opens covers all of
3871	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) if and only if the \(f_j\) generate the unit ideal:
3872	  \(\bigcup_j D(f_j) = \operatorname{Spec} R \iff \operatorname{span}\{f_j\} = R\), since
3873	  the closed complement \(V(\operatorname{span}\{f_j\})\) is empty exactly when the ideal
3874	  is the whole ring.

3903	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof), Tag 01I8.}
3904	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent
3905	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let \(f \in R\). Then, transporting along the canonical
3906	  isomorphism of affine schemes \(D(f) \cong \operatorname{Spec} R_f\), the restriction
3907	  \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(f)}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R_f}\)-module,
3908	  and in particular it admits a presentation
3909	  \(\mathcal{O}^{(J)} \to \mathcal{O}^{(I)} \to \mathcal{F}|_{D(f)} \to 0\) over
3910	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f\). Consequently
3911	  \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(f)} \cong \widetilde{M}\) for the \(R_f\)-module
3912	  \(M = \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\).
3913	\end{lemma}
3914	\begin{proof}
3915	  \uses{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}
3916	  By the quasi-coherence data of \(\mathcal{F}\) there is an open covering
3917	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_i U_i\) and, over each \(U_i\), a presentation of
3918	  \(\mathcal{F}|_{U_i}\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover} this covering
3919	  refines to a finite standard-open covering \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_{j} D(g_j)\)
3920	  with each \(D(g_j) \subseteq U_{\varphi(j)}\); restricting the presentation over
3921	  \(U_{\varphi(j)}\) along the open immersion \(D(g_j) \hookrightarrow U_{\varphi(j)}\)
3922	  (functoriality of presentations under restriction) presents \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(g_j)}\) over
3923	  \(D(g_j) = \operatorname{Spec} R_{g_j}\), so \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(g_j)} \cong \widetilde{M_j}\)
3924	  for an \(R_{g_j}\)-module \(M_j\) by
3925	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}. Now fix \(f\). The basic opens
3926	  \(D(g_j f) = D(g_j) \cap D(f)\) cover \(D(f) = \operatorname{Spec} R_f\), and on each of
3927	  them \(\mathcal{F}\) is the associated sheaf of the localisation \((M_j)_f\)
3928	  (\texttt{lemma-widetilde-pullback}: restricting \(\widetilde{M_j}\) to a standard open gives
3929	  the associated sheaf of the away-localised module). Thus \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(f)}\), viewed as
3930	  an \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R_f}\)-module via \(D(f) \cong \operatorname{Spec} R_f\),
3931	  is locally of the form \(\widetilde{(-)}\), hence quasi-coherent, and the finitely many local
3932	  presentations assemble (over the finite affine cover of \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f\)) to a
3933	  presentation of \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(f)}\). The affine structure theorem then gives
3934	  \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(f)} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})}\) as an \(R_f\)-module.
3935	\end{proof}

3937	\begin{lemma}[\(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) is local on a finite spanning cover]
3938	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover}
3939	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover}
3940	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(M\) and \(N\) be \(R\)-modules, let
3941	  \(g : M \to N\) be an \(R\)-linear map, let \(f \in R\), and let
3942	  \(s : \{1, \ldots, n\} \to R\) be a finite family with
3943	  \(\operatorname{span}\{s_1, \ldots, s_n\} = R\) (the unit ideal). For each \(j\) write
3944	  \(M_{s_j} = (\operatorname{powers} s_j)^{-1} M\) and \(N_{s_j} = (\operatorname{powers}
3945	  s_j)^{-1} N\) for the localisations at the powers of \(s_j\), and let
3946	  \(g_{s_j} : M_{s_j} \to N_{s_j}\) be the induced \(R\)-linear (equivalently
3947	  \(R_{s_j}\)-linear) map. Suppose that for every \(j\) the localised map \(g_{s_j}\)
3948	  exhibits \(N_{s_j}\) as the localisation of \(M_{s_j}\) at the powers of (the image of)
3949	  \(f\), i.e.\ \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g_{s_j}\). Then
3950	  \(g\) itself exhibits \(N\) as the localisation of \(M\) at the powers of \(f\):
3951	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g\).
3952	\end{lemma}
3953	\begin{proof}
3954	  This is a purely commutative-algebra statement: the predicate
3955	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g\) is the conjunction of its
3956	  three defining conditions, and each is verified by descent along the spanning cover. Throughout we use the standard partition
3957	  of unity: since \(\operatorname{span}\{s_j\} = R\), for any chosen exponents \(N_j\) the
3958	  powers \(s_j^{N_j}\) again span the unit ideal, so there are \(r_j \in R\) with
3959	  \(\sum_j r_j s_j^{N_j} = 1\); and an element of an \(R\)-module is zero iff its image in
3960	  \(M_{s_j}\) vanishes for every \(j\) (locality of being zero on a spanning cover, the
3961	  \(s_j\)-torsion form of the same partition of unity).
3962	
3963	  \emph{\(f\) acts invertibly on \(N\).} For each \(j\), the hypothesis
3964	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g_{s_j}\) makes multiplication
3965	  by \(f\) on \(N_{s_j}\) bijective. Multiplication by \(f\) on \(N\) is an isomorphism iff it
3966	  is so after localising at each \(s_j\) (an \(R\)-linear endomorphism is bijective iff it is
3967	  bijective on the cover \(\{D(s_j)\}\), since injectivity and surjectivity are both local on
3968	  a spanning cover); hence \(f \cdot (-) : N \to N\) is bijective, and likewise every power
3969	  \(f^k\).
3970	
3971	  \emph{Every \(y \in N\) satisfies \(f^k y = g(x)\) for some \(x \in M\), \(k\).}
3972	  Fix \(y \in N\). For each \(j\), surjectivity of \(g_{s_j}\) as a localisation at the powers
3973	  of \(f\) yields \(m_j \in M\), exponents \(a_j\) (clearing the \(s_j\)-denominator) and
3974	  \(k_j\) with \(s_j^{a_j} f^{k_j} y = g(m_j)\) holding after localising at \(s_j\); clearing
3975	  the remaining \(s_j\)-torsion (enlarging \(a_j\)) we may take this as an honest equality
3976	  \(s_j^{a_j} f^{k_j} y = g(m_j)\) in \(N\). Choose a common \(k = \max_j k_j\) (replacing
3977	  \(m_j\) by \(f^{k - k_j} m_j\)) so that \(s_j^{a_j} f^{k} y = g(m_j)\) for all \(j\). Pick,
3978	  by the partition of unity, \(r_j\) with \(\sum_j r_j s_j^{a_j} = 1\) (possible because the
3979	  \(s_j^{a_j}\) span the unit ideal). Then
3980	  \(f^{k} y = \sum_j r_j s_j^{a_j} f^{k} y = \sum_j r_j\, g(m_j) = g\bigl(\sum_j r_j m_j\bigr)\),
3981	  so \(x = \sum_j r_j m_j\) and the exponent \(k\) work.
3982	
3983	  \emph{If \(g(x) = g(x')\) then \(f^k(x - x') = 0\) for some \(k\).} Put
3984	  \(z = x - x'\), so \(g(z) = 0\). For each \(j\), the equaliser clause of
3985	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g_{s_j}\) gives, from the
3986	  vanishing of \(g_{s_j}(z) = 0\) in \(N_{s_j}\), an exponent \(k_j\) with
3987	  \(f^{k_j} z = 0\) after localising at \(s_j\); clearing the \(s_j\)-denominator there is
3988	  \(b_j\) with \(s_j^{b_j} f^{k_j} z = 0\) in \(M\). Take \(k = \max_j k_j\), so
3989	  \(s_j^{b_j} f^{k} z = 0\) for all \(j\). With \(r_j\) chosen so that
3990	  \(\sum_j r_j s_j^{b_j} = 1\),
3991	  \(f^{k} z = \sum_j r_j s_j^{b_j} f^{k} z = 0\). Hence \(f^{k}(x - x') = 0\).
3992	
3993	  The three clauses are exactly the data of
3994	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{powers} f)\, g\).
3995	\end{proof}

4036	  Choose, by Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}, a finite basic-open cover

4064	\begin{lemma}[Global generation of a quasi-coherent sheaf on an affine]
4065	  \label{lem:qcoh_global_generation}
4066	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_global_generation}

4068	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Tag 01I8, Lemma
4069	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof) (read from
4070	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1366--1379).
4071	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Explicitly, in the current situation, this boils down to
4072	  % the following argument: Let us construct an $R$-module map
4073	  % $$
4074	  % M \longrightarrow \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}).
4075	  % $$
4076	  % Namely, given $m \in M$ we get $m_i = m/1 \in M_{f_i} = M_i$
4077	  % by construction of $M$. By construction of $M_i$ this corresponds
4078	  % to a section $s_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)$. (Namely, $\varphi^{-1}_i(m_i)$.)"
4079	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Tag 01I8, \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}
4080	  (proof); cf.\ Hartshorne II.5.16--17.}
4081	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
4082	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then \(\mathcal{F}\) is generated by its global sections:
4083	  there is an index set \(I\) of global sections and an epimorphism of
4084	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\).
4085	  Equivalently, \(\mathcal{F}\) carries a global generating family (the data
4086	  \(\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{GeneratingSections}\)).
4087	\end{lemma}
4088	\begin{proof}
4089	  \uses{lem:qcoh_localized_sections, lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}
4090	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover} pick a finite basic-open cover
4091	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_{j=1}^{n} D(f_j)\) on each of which
4092	  \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(f_j)} \cong \widetilde{M_j}\). Each \(M_j\) is generated, as an
4093	  \(R_{f_j}\)-module, by local sections \(t \in \Gamma(D(f_j), \mathcal{F})\). By the
4094	  surjectivity half of Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_localized_sections}, after multiplying by a
4095	  suitable power \(f_j^{N}\) each such local section is the restriction of a global
4096	  section \(s \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\); since \(f_j\) is a unit on \(D(f_j)\), these
4097	  global sections still generate \(\mathcal{F}\) over \(D(f_j)\). Collecting the finitely
4098	  many resulting global sections over all \(j\) gives a family that generates
4099	  \(\mathcal{F}\) on every member of the cover, hence everywhere: the induced map
4100	  \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \to \mathcal{F}\) is surjective on each \(D(f_j)\) and so is an
4101	  epimorphism of sheaves.

4104	\begin{lemma}[The tilde functor preserves kernels]
4105	  \label{lem:tilde_preserves_kernels}
4106	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits}
4107	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, \texttt{lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent}
4108	  % (proof) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1425--1432).
4109	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "This follows from the exactness of the functor $\widetilde{\ }$
4110	  % since by Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions} we know that any map
4111	  % $\psi : \widetilde{M} \to \widetilde{N}$ comes from
4112	  % an $R$-module map $\varphi : M \to N$. (So we have
4113	  % $\Ker(\psi) = \widetilde{\Ker(\varphi)}$ and
4114	  % $\Coker(\psi) = \widetilde{\Coker(\varphi)}$.)"
4115	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes,
4116	  \texttt{lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent} (and Tag 01HV, exactness of
4117	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\)).}
4118	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\). The functor \(M \mapsto \widetilde{M}\) from
4119	  \(R\)-modules to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is exact; in particular it preserves
4120	  kernels: for an \(R\)-module map \(\varphi : M \to N\) the induced
4121	  \(\widetilde{\varphi} : \widetilde{M} \to \widetilde{N}\) has
4122	  \(\operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi}) = \widetilde{\operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)}\),
4123	  and more generally \(\widetilde{(-)}\) carries finite limits of \(R\)-modules to the
4124	  corresponding limits of associated sheaves. The tilde functor is already known to be
4125	  additive and to preserve colimits; its preservation of finite limits (kernels) is the
4126	  property needed here and is supplied as a dedicated lemma.
4127	\end{lemma}
4128	
4129	% NOTE: The Lean instance AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits
4130	% (PreservesFiniteLimits / PreservesKernels for the tilde functor
4131	% ~ : ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules) is ABSENT from Mathlib and is
4132	% project-to-build (Route-P P3 sub-gap). Mathlib provides the tilde functor
4133	% and its stalk computation but not the packaged finite-limit-preservation
4134	% instance; the informal proof below is the stalkwise-flatness argument the
4135	% eventual prover formalizes.
4136	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Let us compute the stalk of $\widetilde M$ at a point
4137	% $x \in \Spec(R)$. Suppose that $x$ corresponds to the prime
4138	% $\mathfrak p \subset R$. By definition of the stalk we see that
4139	% $\widetilde M_x = \colim_{f\in R, f\not\in \mathfrak p} M_f$ ...
4140	% Hence by Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-localization-colimit}
4141	% we conclude that $\widetilde M_x = M_{\mathfrak p}$."
4142	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The exactness of the functor $M \mapsto \widetilde M$
4143	% follows from the fact that the functor $M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak p}$
4144	% is exact and the fact that exactness of short exact sequences
4145	% may be checked on stalks, see Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-abelian}."
4146	\begin{proof}
4147	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (item~(7) and
4148	  proof), Tag 01HV.}
4149	  Kernels in the category of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules are computed
4150	  objectwise and sheafified, and isomorphisms of sheaves may be checked on stalks: a
4151	  morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is an isomorphism if and only if it induces an
4152	  isomorphism on every stalk. So it suffices to compare stalks. By the stalk computation
4153	  for the tilde functor (\texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} item~(7)) the stalk of
4154	  \(\widetilde{M}\) at a point \(x \in \operatorname{Spec} R\) corresponding to the prime
4155	  \(\mathfrak{p}\) is the localisation
4156	  \(\widetilde{M}_x = M_{\mathfrak{p}}\), naturally in \(M\): the stalk functor at \(x\)
4157	  applied to \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is the localisation functor \(M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak{p}}\).
4158	
4159	  Now let \(\varphi : M \to N\) be an \(R\)-module map with kernel
4160	  \(K = \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)\), giving the exact sequence
4161	  \(0 \to K \to M \xrightarrow{\varphi} N\). Localisation \(R \to R_{\mathfrak{p}}\) is
4162	  flat, hence the functor \(M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak{p}}\) is exact and in particular
4163	  preserves kernels: \(K_{\mathfrak{p}} = \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi_{\mathfrak{p}} :
4164	  M_{\mathfrak{p}} \to N_{\mathfrak{p}})\). Translating through the stalk identification,
4165	  the natural comparison map
4166	  \(\widetilde{K} \to \operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi})\) induces, at every point
4167	  \(x \leftrightarrow \mathfrak{p}\), the isomorphism \(K_{\mathfrak{p}} \xrightarrow{\sim}
4168	  \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi_{\mathfrak{p}})\) (using that the stalk functor, being a
4169	  filtered colimit, is itself exact and commutes with forming the kernel). Being a
4170	  stalkwise isomorphism, the comparison map is an isomorphism of sheaves, so
4171	  \(\operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi}) = \widetilde{\operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)}\).
4172	  The same stalkwise-flatness argument applied to a finite diagram of \(R\)-modules shows
4173	  that \(\widetilde{(-)}\) carries finite limits to finite limits; equivalently
4174	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is left exact, i.e.\ preserves finite limits (kernels).
4175	\end{proof}

4177	\begin{lemma}[The kernel of a generating epimorphism is quasi-coherent]
4178	  \label{lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh}

4188	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent
4189	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let \(\sigma : \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{GeneratingSections}\)
4190	  be the generating family of Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_global_generation}, with epimorphism
4191	  \(\sigma.\pi : \mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Then
4192	  \(\operatorname{Ker}(\sigma.\pi)\) is again quasi-coherent on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\),
4193	  and therefore — by Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_global_generation} applied to it — globally
4194	  generated, yielding a second generating family
4195	  \(\tau : (\operatorname{Ker} \sigma.\pi).\mathrm{GeneratingSections}\).
4196	\end{lemma}
4197	\begin{proof}
4198	  \uses{lem:qcoh_global_generation, lem:tilde_preserves_kernels, lem:free_isQuasicoherent}
4199	  The free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)}\) is quasi-coherent

4442	  \label{def:basis_cov_system}

4445	  \textit{Project-bespoke encoding of the hypotheses of Stacks 01EO.}
4446	  A \emph{cover system on a basis} for a scheme \(X\) is a record with five fields. A
4447	  \emph{covering datum} (the helper \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\)) is a pair \((\iota, U)\) of an index type
4448	  \(\iota\) together with an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\); it is the raw shape
4449	  \(\Sigma\,\iota,\ \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) over which the section {\v C}ech complex is built.
4450	  The cover system bundles:
4451	  \begin{enumerate}
4452	  \item a basis \(\mathcal{B} \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) for the topology;
4453	  \item a set \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of admissible coverings, each a \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\);
4454	  \item \emph{faces-in-basis} (\(\mathrm{faces\_mem}\)): for every covering in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) every
4455	    finite intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) of its opens lies in \(\mathcal{B}\) (condition (1) of
4456	    Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis});
4457	  \item \emph{section surjectivity} (\(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\)): for every \(V \in \mathcal{B}\)
4458	    and every short exact sequence \(S : 0 \to S_1 \to S_2 \to S_3 \to 0\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
4459	    whose left term \(S_1\) has vanishing positive {\v C}ech cohomology over the coverings of \(V\) in
4460	    \(\mathrm{Cov}\), the section map \(S_2(V) \to S_3(V)\) is surjective;
4461	  \item \emph{injective acyclicity} (\(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\)): for every injective
4462	    \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\) and every covering \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\) the
4463	    positive-degree {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes, \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for
4464	    all \(p > 0\).
4465	  \end{enumerate}
4466	  Unlike a bare combinatorial record, the structure carries two sheaf-theoretic ({\v C}ech-cohomology)
4467	  fields. The field \(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\) is \emph{not} the raw cofinality datum: it is the
4468	  section-surjectivity \emph{output} that cofinality together with Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1}
4469	  produces, packaged directly as the consequence the 01EO chain consumes. The field
4470	  \(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\) is the injective {\v C}ech-acyclicity of
4471	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} for the system's own coverings. These are exactly the data

4483	  Given a cover system \(s\) on a basis (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system}), an
4484	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for
4485	  \(s\)} when

4487	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad
4488	      \text{for every } \mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov} \text{ and every } p > 0,
4489	  \]
4490	  with \(\check{H}^p\) the accessor of Definition~\ref{def:cech_cohomology_accessor}. This is the
4491	  per-module form of condition (3). It is deliberately stated for an \emph{arbitrary}
4492	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module: the dimension-shift induction of
4493	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} feeds the quotient \(\mathcal{Q} =
4494	  \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\) back as the coefficient, and \(\mathcal{Q}\) need not be quasi-coherent
4495	  even when \(\mathcal{F}\) is. The inductive class is therefore ``\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules with
4496	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\)'', a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) entering only
4497	  as the seed at the affine (Tag 02KG) instantiation; the predicate must \emph{not} be specialized
4498	  to quasi-coherent modules.
4499	\end{definition}
4500	
4501	\begin{lemma}
4502	[Product of short exact sequences in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) (AB4*)]
4503	  \label{lem:short_exact_pi_map}
4504	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.shortExact_piMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pi_π_map_apply}
4505	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
4506	  \textit{Project AB4* lemma (Archon-original infrastructure).}
4507	  Let \((S_j)_{j \in J}\) be a family, indexed by an arbitrary type \(J\), of short exact sequences
4508	  of abelian groups, \(S_j : 0 \to A_j \xrightarrow{f_j} B_j \xrightarrow{g_j} C_j \to 0\). Then
4509	  the short complex assembled from the product maps,
4510	  \[
4511	    0 \to \prod_{j \in J} A_j \xrightarrow{\;\prod_j f_j\;} \prod_{j \in J} B_j
4512	      \xrightarrow{\;\prod_j g_j\;} \prod_{j \in J} C_j \to 0,
4513	  \]
4514	  is again short exact. This is the AB4* exactness-of-products property of \(\mathrm{Ab}\); it is
4515	  the degreewise engine of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}, since each {\v C}ech term is a product
4516	  of basic-open section groups (Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}).
4517	\end{lemma}
4518	\begin{proof}
4519	  Monomorphy of the product map \(\prod_j f_j\) is automatic, as products preserve monomorphisms.
4520	  Exactness at the middle is checked componentwise: an element of \(\prod_j B_j\) annihilated by
4521	  \(\prod_j g_j\) is annihilated in each coordinate \(j\), hence lies in the image of \(f_j\) by
4522	  exactness of \(S_j\), and the resulting coordinatewise preimages assemble to a preimage in
4523	  \(\prod_j A_j\). The one nontrivial point is epimorphy of \(\prod_j g_j\), which is \emph{not} an

4526	  identification of an \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued product with the set-theoretic product of its
4527	  components — to a preimage of the whole tuple. Hence \(\prod_j g_j\) is an epimorphism and the
4528	  displayed sequence is short exact.
4529	\end{proof}

content:
# Strategy

## Goal

Prove `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`),
the protected, frozen-signature target: for `f : X ⟶ S` separated and quasi-compact, `F`
quasi-coherent, `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`, an isomorphism in the weak existence
form `Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F)` (under
`[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`), with `higherDirectImage f i F =
((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`. End-state: zero inline `sorry` in the cone, zero
project axioms, kernel-only axioms.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 02KG `affine_serre_vanishing` (cover-system + affine instantiation) | ACTIVE | ~2–3 | ~200–350 | `SheafOfModules.toSheaf.PreservesEpimorphisms` + `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective`; 009L cofinality. | Cover-system infra UNBLOCKED (family bridge done). Residual = `O_X`-epi local surjectivity (surj_of_vanishing) + `affineCoverSystem` bundle. Top qcoh theorem additionally gated on the 01I8 row below. |
| 01I8 `F≅~(ΓF)` global generation (Route P) | ACTIVE | ~5–8 | ~500–900 | `IsLocalizedModule.mk` span-cover patching; SheafOfModules restriction `D(f)≅Spec R_f` + `Presentation` from `IsQuasicoherent`; `tilde_preserves_kernels` (`PreservesFiniteLimits` of `~`, absent from Mathlib). | Hartshorne II.5.14–17-scale (comparable to P3 ~1200 LOC / P3b ~1500 LOC). `tilde_preserves_kernels` left-exactness is a from-scratch build. Gates the unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` and thus the 02KG top qcoh theorem. |
| P5a vanishing inputs (consume P3b) | ACTIVE | ~3–4 | ~200–400 | 01XJ leaf done; `Hⁿ(f⁻¹V,G)` Ext-bridge + open-immersion/affine vanishing + augmented-Čech resolution. | Ext-by-injective-resolution-of-2nd-arg backing CONFIRMED present (`InjectiveResolution.extEquivCohomologyClass`). |
| P5b comparison assembly | BLOCKED | ~2–3 | ~150–300 | P4 engine + P5a resolution + termwise `f_*`-acyclicity. | Final Route-A assembly of the protected goal. |

## Completed

| Phase | Iters (done@ · used) | LOC | Files | Key results | Reusable techniques | Pitfalls |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 push–pull functor laws | 002 · 2 | ~120 | `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` | `pushPullMap_comp` closing push–pull functoriality | object-form align `simp [Functor.comp_obj]` before `reassoc_of%`; `rawPushPullMap`+`subst`+pentagon | `conjugateEquiv_comp` mate route INFEASIBLE (kernel `whnf` blow-up) |
| P2 `CechNerve`/`CechComplex` | 002 · 1 | ~60 | `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` | `pushPullFunctor`, `coverCechNerve(Aug)`, `CechNerve`, `CechComplex` axiom-clean | `Over.lift`+`.rightOp`+`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj`; terminal augmentation | none |
| P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (Leray, 015E) | 009 · 6 | ~965 | `AcyclicResolution.lean` | `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`, `rightDerivedOneIsoCokerOfAcyclic`, horseshoe `ofShortExact`, dimension shift — axiom-clean | decompose-then-build; two-step `cokernel.mapIso`; `Nat.rec` staircase off `stairGen` | `ShortComplex.mapCyclesIso` WRONG for left-exact functor; `← G.map_comp` silently fails by a mapped-complex term |
| P3 standard-cover Čech vanishing (section form, tilde) | 022 · ~14 | ~1200 | `CechAcyclic.lean` | `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` + `sectionCech_homology_exact` (IsZero homology p≥1) for `F=~M`; L1 tilde-bridge + L3 combinatorial core | accessors DEFEQ (`rfl`); abstract section maps (`set;clear_value`) before `IsLocalizedModule.ext`; target ABSOLUTE section complex | residual qcoh `F≅~ΓF` (01I8) deferred to 02KG consumer; old relative `affine` sorry superseded |
| P3b Čech↔derived bridge bricks | 025 · ~9 | ~1500 | `PresheafCech`,`FreePresheafComplex`,`CechBridge` | `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (free resolution of `O_𝒰`), `ses_cech_h1`, `injective_cech_acyclic` (pos-degree Čech vanishing for injectives) — axiom-clean | op-transport via `opFunctor`+`preadditiveYoneda`; `erw`+`.trans` for defeq-carrier mismatch; Route-B arrow-iso transfer | `maxHeartbeats 2000000` needed; LSP-staleness (trust only `lake env lean`) |
| Absolute-cohomology Form B + 01EO general basis criterion | 028 · 3 | ~600 | `AbsoluteCohomology`,`CechToCohomology` | `jShriekOU`=sheafify(free(yoneda U)); H⁰≅Γ (+naturality); 01EO chain L1–L4+top (`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`); `BasisCovSystem`/`HasVanishingHigherCech` cover-system encoding | Form B kills restriction-preserves-injectives (injective in 2nd Ext arg); cover-local presheaf form; `attribute [local instance] hasExtModules`; AB4* `Epi(Pi.map)` elementwise | `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` absent in Mathlib → carried as explicit hyp (P5a convention); 3 first-attempt iters, no churn |

## Routes

### Route A — acyclic-resolution comparison (CHOSEN)
The augmented Čech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` on `X` (`Cᵖ = ∏ (j_s)_*(F|_{U_s})`) is
(i) a resolution of `F` and (ii) termwise `(pushforward f)`-acyclic. The P4 abstract lemma
gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱf_* F` directly — ONE abstract lemma, NO spectral sequences. Acyclicity
input (ii) reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(affine, qcoh)=0`, supplied by the P3b bridge.

### Route B — two spectral sequences (REJECTED)
Both absent from Mathlib (multi-thousand-LOC); rests on the same `injective_cech_acyclic` brick as A.

### The acyclicity bridge (torsor-free, load-bearing)
Route A's acyclicity reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(Spec A, qcoh)=0` (02KG). The honest
non-circular route is the minimal Čech↔derived bridge: (1) `injective_cech_acyclic`, (2)
`ses_cech_h1`, (3) the dimension-shift `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO) consuming the
standard-cover Čech vanishing of P3 as its condition (3). All three bricks of (1)+(2) are done;
01EO is the next target. This breaks the circular regress: P3 produces standard-cover Čech
vanishing; the bridge lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing.

### Absolute cohomology realization — Ext of the corepresenting object (Form B)
`H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{X.Modules}(jShriekOU U, F)` where `jShriekOU U := sheafify(free(yoneda U))`
is the corepresenting object of `F ↦ Γ(U,F) = F(U)` (`= j_!O_U` up to iso), built from the
already-shipped `freeYonedaHomEquiv` + `sheafificationAdjunction` (~50–80 LOC). The three facts
01EO consumes are then off-the-shelf with the SES staying in `X.Modules`: injective vanishing
`Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (injective `I` as the 2nd arg — no restriction taken), covariant LES
`Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` at fixed 1st arg `jShriekOU U`, and `H⁰ ≅ Γ(U,F)` via the
corepresentability chain. The discarded alternatives: Form A `Ext(O_U, F|_U)` needs
restriction-preserves-injectives (≈ a 200–500 LOC `j_!` functor); `Functor.rightDerived` has
free injective vanishing but no packaged LES; `Sheaf.H` is absent. Reversal signal: Ext
universe/smallness pain over `SheafOfModules` → fall back to Route γ (Čech colimit), never `Sheaf.H`.

## Open strategic questions

- **EnoughInjectives connector (cone obligation).** The frozen target provides
  `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` but the 01EO/02KG cone carries the stronger
  `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]`. Connector `HasInjectiveResolutions C → EnoughInjectives C` is TRUE,
  ~6 LOC (deg-0 mono into `I.cocomplex.X 0` via `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι`).
  Build + register at the P5b assembly lane — DISSOLVES the "EnoughInjectives absent in Mathlib" worry.
- P5a last-mile bridge `Hᵏ((f_*I^•)(V)) = Ext^k(jShriek(f⁻¹V), G)`: Ext-by-injective-resolution-of-2nd-arg
  backing CONFIRMED present (`InjectiveResolution.extEquivCohomologyClass`). Due when P5a consumers dispatch.
- 02KG `surj_of_vanishing` route: `ses_cech_h1` + `O_X`-epi local section surjectivity via
  `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective` (`isLocallySurjective_iff`), gated on one gap-fill
  `SheafOfModules.toSheaf.PreservesEpimorphisms`; refine the lift cover to affine opens
  (`Scheme.isBasis_affineOpens`) to land a standard cover in `Cov`.
- 01I8 Route P (global generation, Hartshorne II.5.14–17 / Stacks 01I8): the `\uses`-chain P0–P4
  (`rem:o1i8_decomposition`) — P0 `exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover` → P1 `qcoh_localized_sections`
  (`Γ(D(f),F)=Γ(X,F)_f`, load-bearing) = P1a (affine restriction infra `D(f)≅Spec R_f` + `Presentation`
  from `IsQuasicoherent F`) + P1b (`IsLocalizedModule` local-on-span-cover via `IsLocalizedModule.mk`) +
  sheaf condition → P2 `qcoh_global_generation` → P3 `qcoh_kernel_qcoh` (needs `tilde_preserves_kernels`)
  → P4 `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` (feed P2+P3 to the done `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections`);
  conditional + presentation + genSections forms already landed. Route G (module gluing) rejected (no
  `Module.GlueData`; FF descent a Mathlib TODO).

## Mathlib gaps & new material

Gaps to fill (project-side, Mathlib lacks):
- Standard-cover Čech complex exactness `0→M→∏M_{f_i}→⋯` (P3) — done via L1+L3 around `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
- Presheaf-level Čech machinery for `O_X`-modules (P3b free/section complexes, `injective_cech_acyclic`,
  `ses_cech_h1`) — done. Dropped as off-critical-path: presheaf enough-injectives + δ-functor universality.
- Absolute module-valued `Hⁿ(U,F)` = `Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` via `Abelian.Ext` (Form B); corepresenting
  object `jShriekOU = sheafify(free(yoneda U))`. NOT a bespoke `j_!` functor (that is absent, 200–500 LOC).
- Relative/open-immersion affine vanishing `R^q(jₛ)_*=0`, `R^i(affine)_*=0` (P5).
- EnoughInjectives connector `HasInjectiveResolutions C → EnoughInjectives C` (~6 LOC, instance) — see
  Open questions; makes the 01EO/02KG cone type-check against the frozen target's weaker hypothesis.
- `SheafOfModules.toSheaf.PreservesEpimorphisms` (epi of `O_X`-modules ⇒ epi of underlying abelian sheaf) —
  small project-local instance unlocking `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective` for `surj_of_vanishing`.
- 01I8 instance `[IsQuasicoherent F] → IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` via Route P (global generation): P1
  `qcoh_localized_sections` (`Γ(D(f),F)=Γ(X,F)_f`) = P1a (SheafOfModules restriction-to-`D(f)` ≅ `Spec R_f`
  + `Presentation` of the restriction from `IsQuasicoherent`) + P1b (`IsLocalizedModule` local-on-span-cover,
  via `IsLocalizedModule.mk`); P3 sub-gap `tilde_preserves_kernels` (`PreservesFiniteLimits` of `~`, absent
  in Mathlib). ~few-hundred LOC.

New project material:
- push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `p ↦ p_* p^* F` — hand-rolled.
- `CechNerve`/`CechComplex`; `AcyclicResolution.lean` (P4); P3b free-presheaf + section Čech complexes,
  `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`, `injective_cech_acyclic`, `ses_cech_h1`.
- `AbsoluteCohomology.lean`: `jShriekOU`, corepresentability iso, `H^p := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, -)`, H⁰=Γ,
  injective-vanishing + LES wrappers → 01EO `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` → 02KG `affine_serre_vanishing`.
- comparison assembly `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` via Route A.

# Blueprint Review Report

## Slug
iter032

## Iteration
032

## Top-level summaries

### Dependency & isolation findings

- `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` / `lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`: `\uses{def:basis_cov_system}` appears in both the statement block and the proof block. The proof ("the forgetful functor is exact because it has both adjoints…") is purely categorical and does not involve `BasisCovSystem` at all. The `\lean{}` target `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms` is a category-level instance on sheaves of modules, not parameterized by a cover system. **wire-up** — remove `\uses{def:basis_cov_system}` from both blocks; the lemma has no project-internal dependencies. (soon severity — does not affect prover correctness, since Lean doesn't enforce `\uses{}` ordering.)

- `lean:AlgebraicGeometry.…` (1 isolated lean_aux node): Lean auxiliary helper with no blueprint entry and no blueprint edges. **keep** — lean_aux nodes are uncovered Lean helpers, not blueprint scaffolding. No action required.

- `lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy` and `lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec` (2 blueprint nodes, `leandag show gaps`): No `\lean{}` hint on either. Both carry explicit `% NOTE` comments: these are transport-step descriptions, not standalone Lean declarations; the content is realized by transporting `cechEnginePrepend` / `cechEnginePrepend_spec` across `cechFreeEvalEngineIso`. **keep** — the design decision is documented and correct; no action required.

## Per-chapter

### blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_HigherDirectImage.tex — complete + correct, no notes.

### blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_AcyclicResolution.tex — complete + correct, no notes.

### blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex
- **complete**: partial (expected — AffineSerreVanishing, QcohTildeSections, P5a/P5b blocks are active/blocked phases; formalization ongoing)
- **correct**: true
- **notes**:
  - All blocks in AffineSerreVanishing focus area are formalize-ready (see Focus-area 1 below).
  - P1b `lem:isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover` is formalize-ready (see Focus-area 2 below).
  - P1a `lem:isQuasicoherent_restrict_basicOpen` and `lem:tilde_preserves_kernels` are well-formed, properly `% NOTE`-flagged project-to-build; not dispatched this iter. ✓
  - `lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi` has a spurious `\uses{def:basis_cov_system}` — soon finding, see above.

---

## Focus-area 1: AffineSerreVanishing.lean gate

Blocks reviewed: `lem:affine_faces_mem`, `lem:standard_cover_cofinal`, `lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing`, `lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`, `def:affine_cover_system`, `def:basis_cov_system`, `lem:cover_datum_bridge`.

**`lem:affine_faces_mem`** (`AlgebraicGeometry.affine_faces_mem`)
- Statement: D(f)∩D(g) = D(fg) for Spec R; finite intersections of a standard cover's opens are again distinguished opens. This is the `faces_mem` field (condition 1) of the affine cover system.
- `\uses{}`: `def:standard_affine_cover` (mathlibok). Correct.
- Proof: D(f)∩D(g) = D(fg) by prime avoidance; iterate for arbitrary face. Proof block `\leanok`. ✓
- **FORMALIZE-READY.**

**`lem:standard_cover_cofinal`** (`AlgebraicGeometry.standard_cover_cofinal`)
- Statement: Standard covers of D(f) are cofinal among all open coverings of D(f): every open covering of D(f) admits a refinement that is a standard open cover. This is the cofinality input for Tag 009L.
- `\uses{}`: `def:standard_affine_cover`, `lem:scheme_isBasis_affineOpens` (both mathlibok). Proof block also uses `lem:affine_faces_mem`. Consistent.
- Proof: D(f) is quasi-compact; basic opens form a basis (lem:scheme_isBasis_affineOpens) → refine any covering to finite standard cover. Closure under intersection (lem:affine_faces_mem) establishes the 009L cofinality condition. Adequate for formalization.
- **FORMALIZE-READY.**

**`lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing`** (`AlgebraicGeometry.affine_surj_of_vanishing`)
- Statement: Let V be a distinguished open and 0→S₁→S₂→S₃→0 a SES of O_X-modules with Ȟᵖ(𝒰, S₁)=0 for every standard cover 𝒰 of V and p>0. Then S₂(V)→S₃(V) is surjective.
- `\uses{}`: `lem:ses_cech_h1`, `lem:standard_cover_cofinal`, `lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`, `lem:presheaf_is_locally_surjective`, `lem:sheaf_locally_surjective_iff_epi`, `lem:scheme_isBasis_affineOpens`. All correct; all prior-done inputs (ses_cech_h1, injective_cech_acyclic axiom-clean from iter-024/025; mathlib items present).
- Proof: Detailed 3-step argument. Step 1: g: S₂→S₃ is an epi of O_X-modules → underlying abelian-sheaf epi (lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi) → locally surjective (lem:sheaf_locally_surjective_iff_epi + lem:presheaf_is_locally_surjective). Step 2: refine the local-surjectivity covering to a standard cover using lem:scheme_isBasis_affineOpens + lem:standard_cover_cofinal. Step 3: glue via Ȟ¹(𝒰, S₁)=0 using lem:ses_cech_h1.
- **FORMALIZE-READY.**

**`lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`** (`AlgebraicGeometry.toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms`)
- Statement: The forgetful functor from O_X-modules to underlying abelian sheaves preserves epimorphisms.
- `\uses{}`: `def:basis_cov_system` (spurious — proof is pure category theory, no cover system). See soon finding above.
- Proof: The forgetful functor is exact (left adjoint composed with exact sheafification–forgetful) → sends the cokernel of g to the cokernel of its image → zero cokernel preserved → epi preserved.
- Statement is mathematically correct. Spurious `\uses{}` is a graph-cleanliness issue, not a math error.
- **FORMALIZE-READY** (the spurious \uses{} does not block the prover).

**`def:affine_cover_system`** (`AlgebraicGeometry.affineCoverSystem`)
- Statement: The affine cover system for Spec R: basis = distinguished opens D(f), admissible coverings = finite standard open covers of a distinguished open. Fields: faces_mem ← lem:affine_faces_mem; surj_of_vanishing ← lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing; injective_acyclic ← lem:injective_cech_acyclic (family-form, axiom-clean, applies to standard covers of any D(f)).
- `\uses{}`: `def:basis_cov_system`, `lem:affine_faces_mem`, `lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing`, `lem:injective_cech_acyclic`. Correct and complete.
- Note: `lem:injective_cech_acyclic` (family form, from CechBridge iter-025) is already axiom-clean. The `% NOTE` makes this explicit.
- **FORMALIZE-READY.**

**`def:basis_cov_system`** (`AlgebraicGeometry.BasisCovSystem, AlgebraicGeometry.CovDatum`)
- Per iter-028 memory notes, `BasisCovSystem` is already formalized (axiom-clean, part of the 01EO full chain). Not in `unmatched_lean`. ✓
- **ALREADY DONE.**

**`lem:cover_datum_bridge`** (`AlgebraicGeometry.coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan`)
- Statement: For a standard affine cover from a spanning family s, coverOpen 𝒰 i = D(sᵢ) in Opens(Spec R).
- `\uses{}`: `def:standard_affine_cover`. Correct.

## Focus-area 2: QcohTildeSections.lean — P1b `lem:isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`

**Statement** (`AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`):
- R commutative ring; M, N R-modules; g: M→N R-linear; f∈R; s: {1,…,n}→R with span(range s) = R.
- For each j: the map g_{sⱼ}: M_{sⱼ}→N_{sⱼ} (induced by g after localizing at powers of sⱼ) satisfies `IsLocalizedModule (powers f) g_{sⱼ}`.
- Conclusion: `IsLocalizedModule (powers f) g`.

**Correctness check**: The three `IsLocalizedModule` fields are:
1. f acts invertibly on N (every f^k: N→N bijective).
2. Every y∈N satisfies f^k·y = g(x) for some x∈M, k∈ℕ.
3. g(x)=g(x') implies f^k(x−x')=0 for some k∈ℕ.

All three follow from the local hypotheses via partition of unity (the sⱼ span the unit ideal). The statement is **mathematically correct**. The localization-of-a-module-map setup (`IsLocalizedModule (powers f) g_{sⱼ}` where g_{sⱼ}: M_{sⱼ}→N_{sⱼ}) is a standard Lean/Mathlib predicate application and is well-formed.

**Self-containedness**: All hypotheses are explicit. No implicit assumptions beyond standard Mathlib algebra. No `\uses{}` needed (pure commutative algebra over Mathlib's `IsLocalizedModule`). Correct to have no `\uses{}` for this block.

**Proof sketch adequacy for the three `IsLocalizedModule.mk` fields**:

- **Field 1 (f invertible on N)**: "Multiplication by f on N is an isomorphism iff it is so after localising at each sⱼ (injectivity and surjectivity are both local on a spanning cover)." — correct and sufficient. The locality of bijectivity on a spanning cover is a standard algebra fact.

- **Field 2 (surjectivity up to f-power)**: Explicit partition-of-unity construction: for each j, clearing sⱼ-denominator and f-denominator yields sⱼ^{aⱼ}·f^k·y = g(mⱼ); choose common k; sum with rⱼ from ∑rⱼsⱼ^{aⱼ}=1 gives f^k·y = g(∑rⱼmⱼ). Fully detailed.

- **Field 3 (equalizer)**: Set z = x−x' with g(z)=0; local IsLocalizedModule gives sⱼ^{bⱼ}·f^{kⱼ}·z=0 in M; take k=max kⱼ; sum with rⱼ from ∑rⱼsⱼ^{bⱼ}=1 gives f^k·z=0. Fully detailed.

All three clauses are worked out at a level that directly maps to `IsLocalizedModule.mk` in Lean. The key intermediate steps (locality of bijectivity, partition-of-unity argument, denominators-clearing via exact equalities in M) are all spelled out.

**HARD GATE — P1b `lem:isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`: CLEARS.**
